meaning: adj.大陆的,大陆性的n.欧洲人
examples: He sees no signs of improvement in the U.K. and continental economy.
Prices are often higher in Hawaii than in the continental United States.
The most ancient parts of the continental crust are 4000 million years old.
The strong sterling means British buyers are crossing the channel in increasing numbers, Continental brokers say.
Southwest Airlines, Delta, and United Continental all rallying between 20% and 30% in that time.
